skip_reason: The bookmarklet works by scanning inline <script> tags on the page for an already-embedded VK API 'users.get' JSON response containing a 'last_seen' field. This embedded API call is part of VK's server-rendered profile bootstrap data, which VK typically only includes for sessions with an authenticated/logged-in viewer (and may vary by locale/A-B test). For an anonymous visitor the expected script content is unlikely to be present, so the bookmarklet would just alert('Could not extract user data') rather than exercise the real modal. No reliable way to verify this works anonymously in CI.
notes: Passive - regex-scans document <script> tags for a 'users.get' / 'last_seen' JSON blob, parses it as userData, and renders a draggable modal (header text '✋🏼 Click here to move window' / 'Generated by a bookmarklet from https://tools.myosint.training', no separate <h2> title) showing VK ID, Name, Nickname, Birthdate, Gender, Location, Last Seen, etc. plus a status badges row (DEAD/MOBILE/NFT/ONLINE) and Text/JSON/Excel export buttons. On failure to find the data it calls alert('Could not extract user data'); on any other error it calls alert('Error: '+e.message).

checks.comment: Modal content should include the 'VK ID' field label if the embedded API data was found
